cards.back: The properties of regular languages include: (1) concatenation, (2) Kleene star, (3) union, (4) Kleene plus, (5) complement, and (6) closure under empty strings

Regular languages are equivalent if they accept the same set of strings, and two regular languages can be equivalent if they can be transformed into each other using the operations of union, concatenation, and Kleene star
Minimization of finite automata involves simplifying the automaton by removing redundant states, and the resulting minimized automaton is said to be equivalent to the original one

Regular expressions are equivalent if they match the same set of strings, and two regular expressions can be equivalent if they can be transformed into each other using the operations of union, concatenation, and Kleene star
Regular expressions satisfy the following closure properties: (1) concatenation, (2) Kleene star, (3) union, and (4) Kleene plus
